content.full_text:
May 3, 1990

Kathryn Donovan  
General Counsel  
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
428 J. Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Ms. Donovan:

I am requesting advice on behalf of Councilmember Shirley Lewis with regards to State disclosure and disqualification obligations which may arise from an elected official's status as a limited partner in a real estate investment partnership.

The partnership is comprised of two general partners and approximately 15 limited partners. The limited partners each have more than $2000 invested in the partnership but less than a 10% aggregate interest in the partnership.

The partnership was created to make Stockton, California area investments. It has a license to do business in Stockton but not in San Jose. Activities for this partnership which consist only of handling correspondence and bookkeeping for the Stockton area properties are handled at the address of a separate partnership in San Jose comprised of the same general partners. The Councilmember has no connection with the San Jose partnership. All real estate investments are physically located in the Stockton, California area.

Both general partners are active in real estate investments in the City of San Jose as individuals and not as general partners of the Stockton partnership. The limited partners have no financial or investment related connection with the general partners' individual investments within the City.

Kathryn Donovan  
May 3, 1990  
Page 2

I will be happy to provide you with any additional information which you will need to provide the requested advice. Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Yours truly,

JOAN R. GALLO,
City Attorney

By: [signature]
NORMAN SATO
Sr. Deputy City Attorney

JRG:NS:g1k

cc: Councilmember Shirley Lewis
